paper_title: Sparse Autoencoders Find Highly Interpretable Features in Language Models
content: We train sparse autoencoders on MLP layer activations to decompose them into a dictionary of interpretable features, each corresponding to a monosemantic concept.

paper_title: Sparse Autoencoders Find Highly Interpretable Features in Language Models
content: Learned dictionary features correspond to recognizable concepts such as DNA, programming syntax, and legal language, with high human interpretability ratings.
Scaling sparse autoencoders to larger dictionaries substantially improves feature interpretability scores while maintaining reconstruction quality.
Current evaluation methods for feature interpretability rely on automated scoring that may not fully capture human judgement of concept meaningfulness.

paper_title: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning
content: Neural network features may represent multiple concepts simultaneously (superposition) when the number of features exceeds the number of neurons, explaining polysemanticity.
